methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Hertha Metals, a Series A-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Laureen Meroueh, has raised $17M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable across similar and adjacent stages, including Foundation Robotics ($3.0B, Series A), 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M, Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $17M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression, yields $34M. Peer-median analysis across the 7-company same-sector, same/adjacent-stage cohort yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in stage advancement and capital intensity within the robotics sector.

The triangulated range spans $29M (floor) to $1.9B (ceiling), with a median of $842M. This wide band reflects material divergence between stage-based and peer-median methods. Key uncertainties include whether Hertha's technology maturity and go-to-market traction align with earlier-stage or later-stage peer benchmarks, and whether the peer set adequately reflects differences in manufacturing capability, addressable market, or path to profitability relative to the target.
